text.title: ES Projections Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed in projection implementations. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Subscription redelivers an event after reconnect; handler does `INSERT` and trips a unique-key violation OR creates a duplicate row.
antipattern.root-cause: Handler assumed exactly-once delivery; used raw INSERT.
antipattern.fix: Convert to UPSERT (`INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` / `MERGE`) keyed by event identity or stream position. Add a contract test redelivering the same event twice.

antipattern.symptom: Projection handler `if order.total > 1000: send_high_value_alert(order)`. After a quarterly rebuild from offset 0, the alert fires for every historical high-value order.
antipattern.fix: Move side effect to a process manager / saga subscribed to events. Projection only writes to its read store.

antipattern.symptom: Service restart processes all events again. Read model is fine due to UPSERT, but startup takes hours; downstream lag alerts fire.
antipattern.fix: Add `projection_checkpoint(name, stream_position)` table. Record after each successful batch. Resume from it on startup.

antipattern.symptom: Projection schema changes; team can't rebuild from log because there's no rebuild script. Read model carries stale data forever.
antipattern.root-cause: No `rebuild-<name>` CLI command; rebuild never exercised.
antipattern.fix: Write `rebuild-<name>` script: truncate read table + checkpoint, replay events from offset 0. Add quarterly CI job that runs rebuild and diffs the result.
